input_dna_matches.expected_link: via Chana Raskes / Raskes-Friedman maternal side
supports Friedman-Brest cluster, not survivor I5
weak Levitin collateral clue; endogamy corrected
insufficient to merge I4 with survivor candidate I5

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: David Friedman, born 1948 in Munich; mother Chana Raskes Friedman, born Brest-Litovsk; father listed as Moshe Friedman, deceased during war. Registration place Munich DP camp.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms DP registration and identifies Munich as registration/birth context; parent names support I2-I3-I4.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Moshe Friedman, son of Leib, husband of Chana, resident of Brisk, murdered 1942. Submitter: Chana Friedman, wife.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: strong victim identity clue; not a civil death certificate but high relevance due to spouse submitter.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: The Friedman, Raskes, and Levitin families are remembered among the Brisk martyrs. Several Moshe Friedman names appear.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: locality/community clue only; cannot infer exact parent-child links.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Chana Friedman, widow, traveling with son David Friedman. Last permanent residence: Munich, Germany. Birthplace: Brest-Litovsk.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms migration path and widow status; does not prove exact death record.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Moshe Friedman, born 1911 Pruzhany, wife Bella, arrived 1923, naturalized 1956.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: separates survivor candidate I5 from victim I4.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Moshe Friedman born Brest 1913, died New York 1981, married Chana Raskes. Also father of David born Munich.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: conflicting copied tree that incorrectly merges I4 and I5.

input_archive_snippets.place: Brest Regional Archive / Jewish civil records
input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Search performed for Moshe Friedman civil death 1941-1944 in surviving local civil registers; no record found. Record loss likely due to ghetto liquidation and wartime destruction.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: negative evidence with documented search; supports record-loss context, not proof by itself.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Moshe Friedman survived and died in New York in 1981
Birthplace should be Munich DP Camp
Family descends from a famous rabbinical line through unknown daughter
input_user_assertions.evidence: Yizkor book mentions rabbi with similar surname

embedded_errors.reason: similar name and age but different birthplace, spouse, immigration path, and death status
no civil death record found after documented search should not disprove Holocaust death

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify I4/I5 as same-name candidates with conflicting death/life events.
Step 2: Parse DP camp record src_501 and immigration manifest src_504; distinguish event place/residence from biological birthplace when required.
Step 3: Correlate Yad Vashem Page of Testimony src_502 with spouse submitter Chana and father Leib; assign strong identity evidence to I4.
Step 4: Treat Yizkor book src_503 as community/locality evidence only; block inferred parent-child or famous-rabbi bridge.
Step 5: Use naturalization record src_505 to separate survivor I5 from victim I4.
Step 6: Apply Holocaust-specific fabrication patterns: perished-vs-survived duplicate, DP-camp field mapping error, Yizkor overreach.
Step 7: Evaluate documented negative search src_507 as record-loss context, not disproof of death.
Step 8: Use DNA matches dna_501 and dna_502 to support Raskes/Friedman branch; apply Ashkenazi endogamy correction to dna_503/dna_504.
Step 9: Confirm Chana Raskes as mother of David and widow of Moshe; keep exact death details as Probable due to lack of primary civil death.
Step 10: Reject merged identity I4=I5 and quarantine public-tree claim src_506.

identity_I4_Moshe_Friedman_Brest_victim.reason: Yad Vashem spouse-submitted testimony + DP record + immigration widow status; no civil death record survives

identity_I5_Moshe_Friedman_New_York_surv.reason: naturalization record with distinct birthplace/spouse/arrival path

birthplace_I2_Munich_DP_Camp.reason: DP registration may indicate birth/registration context; requires parsing event field carefully

raskes_friedman_branch.reason: DNA cluster + DP/immigration records; older Brest records remain research target

true_lineage: I3 Chana Raskes + I4 Moshe Friedman
I6 Freida Raskes + I7 Yankel Raskes
I8 Leib Friedman + I9 Sura Levitin
true_events: I4 Moshe Friedman died during Holocaust in Brest/Brisk area, exact civil death record absent.
I5 Moshe Friedman is a different survivor from Pruzhany/New York.
I2 David Friedman was registered in Munich DP context; birthplace parsing must not overwrite all identity evidence without field validation.
false_claims: I4 and I5 are the same person.
Yizkor book surname list proves direct parent-child relationships.
Unknown Daughter Famous Rabbi bridge is real.
No civil death record means Moshe did not die in Holocaust.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must reject merge of Holocaust victim I4 with New York survivor I5.
Engine must classify Yizkor source as locality/community evidence, not direct kinship proof.
Engine must use documented no-record-found as record-loss context, not disproof.
Engine must confirm I2 as child of I3 and I4 using DP/immigration/DNA evidence.
Engine must reject famous-rabbinical bridge through I10.
